title: City Council calls for more transparency from Wu over transportation projects
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council requests transparency from Mayor Wu on transportation projects amid concerns of delays due to her approval policy.
Councilor Liz Breadon warns $200 million in transportation investments, including MBTA lines, are at risk if delays continue.
Councilor Sharon Durkan urges a comprehensive update to transportation goals, citing old plans and lack of updates since 2017.

title: ‘All the negativity that’s in this town sucks’: Revisiting Rick Pitino’s iconic Celtics rant
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Rick Pitino's Celtics rant on March 1, 2000 highlighted his frustration with the team's struggles and media negativity.
Pitino's tenure as Celtics coach ended with a record of 102-146 after three-and-a-half seasons, resigning in January 2001.
His rant included references to legends Bird, McHale, and Parish, stating they wouldn't return to help the struggling team.

title: Joe Mazzulla’s ‘tough love’ reminds Robert Parish of a particular Hall-of-Fame coach
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Robert Parish sees similarities between current Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla and former coach Bill Fitch in their 'tough love' approach.
Parish appreciates how the Celtics embody Mazzulla's tough persona, which was influenced by Mazzulla's enthusiasm for martial arts.
Parish praised Mazzulla's people skills for keeping reserve players motivated, contributing to the Celtics' team success.

title: Lukas Reichel has a tall task sparking dormant part of Bruins’ lineup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Lukas Reichel, a 2020 first-round pick, joins the Boston Bruins' lineup for the first time on Thursday.
The 23-year-old winger aims to boost the Bruins' slumping third line alongside Morgan Geekie and Elias Lindholm.
Reichel was acquired from Vancouver for a 2026 sixth-round pick and scored five points in three AHL games with Providence.

title: A Massachusetts police officer is running the Boston Marathon for veterans
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Laicey Ieronimo, a Massachusetts police officer, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Semper Fi & America's Fund.
Ieronimo, 25, from Carver, is participating in her third charity marathon, honoring veterans including her family members.
Besides running, Ieronimo is a coach and nonprofit founder, having raised over $40,000 for various charitable causes.

title: Cambridge Day founder Marc Levy is back with a new Cambridge and Somerville news hub
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Marc Levy launches Cambridge Somerville Independent to fill a local news gap, debuting online February 12.
Levy founded Cambridge Day in 2009 and now starts a new venture with a small team of writers and photographers.
Cambridge Somerville Independent operates as a hybrid model with both commercial and nonprofit elements, supported by the Somerville Media Fund.

title: For this Boston Marathon runner, the starting line is everything
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Gregory Baxter, a Marine veteran from Laguna Niguel, Calif., is running the Boston Marathon to honor resilience.
Baxter, 43, aims to support service members through Semper Fi & America's Fund and promote resilience awareness.
The starting line symbolizes Baxter's journey of personal growth, making it more significant than the finish line.

title: Boston City Council to weigh non-citizen voting
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council considers allowing non-citizens with legal status to vote in municipal elections.
Councilor Julia Mejia introduced the proposal, citing increased anti-immigrant rhetoric as a driving force.
Effort faces challenges as similar petitions stalled in the state Legislature previously.

title: ‘He looks great’: Draymond Green had high praise for Jayson Tatum
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Draymond Green praised Jayson Tatum's performance upon returning from injury, noting his movement and basketball skills were impressive.
Jayson Tatum scored 24 points and grabbed 10 rebounds in the Celtics' 120-99 win over the Warriors.
Despite injuries, Tatum's playing ability remained intact, as observed by former Olympic teammate Draymond Green.

title: Two-time cancer survivor from Easton to run Boston Marathon for Mass General
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Kevin O'Rourke, a 57-year-old from Easton, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor his late father and Mass General Hospital.
O'Rourke, a two-time cancer survivor, is participating to celebrate personal recovery and acknowledge the hospital's role in his family's life.
His father, Brian O'Rourke, received life-extending cancer treatment at Mass General, living 26 more years before passing in 2025.
